changes.before: A city this large should have pushed sound upward in a constant body.
changes.after: Twenty-five thousand people, market bells, cart wheels, chapel bells, dogs, and alley arguments should have pushed sound upward in one constant body.

changes.before: Burned now, charred and bitter, but under it the same wrong sweetness that had ridden out of Andorhal in sacks and carts and ordinary kitchens, lying dormant until ovens and hands and hunger did half the work for it.
changes.after: Burned now, charred and bitter, but under it the same wrong sweetness from Andorhal grain, sacks, carts, and ordinary kitchens, lying dormant until ovens and hands and hunger did half the work for it.

changes.before: The deeper pattern only showed itself once Stratholme was beyond saving.
changes.after: Jaina could read the deeper pattern only after Stratholme was beyond saving.

changes.before: Her fingers tightened on the parapet.
changes.after: Jaina tightened her grip on the parapet.
Jaina dug her nails into the stone.

changes.before: The hand stayed where it was. Weight.
changes.after: Sylvanas kept her hand there. Weight.

changes.before: The offer landed with all the weight of home.
changes.after: She felt the full weight of home in Antonidas's offer.

changes.before: Jaina knew that by the way the elf's shoulders changed before any sound reached human ears.
changes.after: Cyndia heard her first; the elf's shoulders changed before any sound reached human ears.

changes.before: The shape had already been there in the road, in the unfinished logic of the plague.
changes.after: She had already begun to read the road as north.

observations.note: Line 7 now names people, bells, carts, dogs, and alley arguments as the sound source instead of laundering the motion into 'city'.
Line 17 now grounds the Andorhal carry-chain in grain, sacks, carts, and kitchens instead of making sweetness travel.
Line 27 now makes Jaina the reader of the pattern, which restores POV agency.
Line 13 shifts the parapet grip back onto Jaina rather than her fingers acting by themselves.
Line 73 now has Jaina dig her nails into stone, removing body-part agency.
Line 97 now makes Sylvanas keep the hand in place, preserving her clipped pressure without the detached body-part subject.
Line 191 now routes the impact of Antonidas's offer through Jaina receiving it.
Line 279 removes the filter construction around Cyndia's early warning and leaves the physical cue doing the work.
The narrator-caption fragment 'Recognition.' is gone.
Line 303 now cashes out the road inference into north instead of placeholder nouns like 'shape' and 'logic'.
Re-read lines 5-8 against intent-smear and recurring-tics; no new inanimate-subject agency remains in the city-noise paragraph.
Re-read lines 5-8 against negation guidance; the paragraph stays affirmative and does not fall back into contrastive not-X scaffolding.
The revised opening paragraph adds no eye/gaze beat and keeps the sensory emphasis on sound, smoke, and impact.
The parapet fix stays in Jaina's physical register and does not introduce document-language or explanatory gloss.
The Andorhal sentence now keeps the transport chain concrete and does not reintroduce anthropomorphic spread language.
The deeper-pattern sentence stays inside Jaina's thinking and does not slide back into narrator commentary.
The nail fix keeps the force bodily and concrete; no new tic or abstract locomotion appears in the surrounding lines.
The Sylvanas shoulder line still sounds like Sylvanas pressure in Jaina POV rather than an editor paraphrase.
The Antonidas-offer line remains personal and humane; no clinical or bureaucratic vocabulary enters the exchange.
The rider-arrival paragraph now lets shoulders and bird-calls carry urgency instead of a knowledge filter.
Removing 'Recognition.' cuts the verdict-tag fragment without weakening the beat transition into Northrend.
The north-reading line now gives the passage a usable directional payload, satisfying the phantom-concreteness complaint.
No changed passage introduced fake simplification; spoken lines still cash out into roads, riders, grain, bodies, and direction.
No changed passage introduced new eye/gaze vocabulary; the chapter stays away from the common revision-mode failure of defaulting to looks and glances.
Re-read every touched passage against the hitlist sections for narrator seep, negation, document voice, and inanimate-agency; no secondary fixes were needed.
